bottom_line: Hardcoded migration-revision constants in integration tests cause S10 to fail on every CR that adds a DB migration; adopt a runtime fixture so the gate passes on first try.
coverage_notes: All logs < 1 MB — read in full. S10 log1 read via tail-100 + grep for errors; S10 log4/log5 read via tail-50. DB:UP confirmed via iw db-identity check; step status confirmed via iw item-status CR-00065 --json.

findings.title: Integration tests encode hardcoded migration-revision constants — fragile across every DB-migration CR
findings.recommendation: Adopt Strategy A (preferred): Add a pytest fixture `current_head_revision` that reads the actual Alembic head revision at runtime. Tests asserting against pending migrations should use `alembic history --limit 1 --style=plain` to discover the head dynamically, not a hardcoded constant. Also add a `make update-test-constants` command for parametric UI assertion counts.
evidence: tests/integration/daemon/test_phase2_apply_no_self_deadlock.py:134 — `_HEAD_REVISION = "e45b45f74ea0"` hardcoded; CR-00065's migration `00490acc4cdf` replaced it; 2 tests failed
tests/dashboard/test_item_steps_table_render.py:373 — `assert header_count == 11` hardcoded; CR-00065's Logs column bumped to 12; 1 test failed
tests/integration/test_dashboard_remaining.py:420 — text assertion not scoped to the correct DOM region; unrelated 'Clear chat' button caused failure
S10 ran 5 times (run1 fail, run2 fail, run3 fail, run4 pass, run5 pass) — ~67 min wall-clock on gate alone
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add runtime pytest fixture `current_head_revision` that reads the Alembic head dynamically; also fix hardcoded UI assertion counts to be parametric. analyzed in CR-00065 (see ai-dev/work/CR-00065/reports/CR-00065_self_assess_report.md). Target files: tests/integration/daemon/test_phase2_apply_no_self_deadlock.py, tests/dashboard/test_item_steps_table_render.py, tests/integration/test_dashboard_remaining.py. Effort: M.

findings.title: S11 browser verification spent ~45 min in environment setup before running actual verification
findings.recommendation: Add a pre-flight check in the qv-browser prompt that detects whether a healthy E2E stack already exists before invoking the rebuild sequence. If `docker ps --filter "name=iw-ai-core-e2e-" --format "{{.Status}}"` returns "healthy" for all required services, skip the full teardown+rebuild. Use incremental `docker compose up` for partial failures.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/CR-00065_S11_browser_env_down.log — E2E stack torn down for unrelated reason
ai-dev/logs/CR-00065_S11_browser_env_up.log — full Docker build + container startup (~385s of Docker build output)
ai-dev/logs/CR-00065_S11_run3.log — actual browser verification completed in ~3 min; setup consumed ~45 min total
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add pre-flight E2E health check to qv-browser prompt; skip full stack rebuild if services are already healthy. analyzed in CR-00065 (see ai-dev/work/CR-00065/reports/CR-00065_self_assess_report.md). Target file: prompts/CR-00065_S11_BrowserVerification_prompt.md (add health-check section). Effort: S.

findings.title: QV gate (S02) ran 3 times with 2 identical passes — spurious re-trigger wastes DB slots and agent time
findings.recommendation: Add logging in `qv_gate_validator.py` that captures why a QV gate is being re-launched. If the worktree hasn't changed since the last pass (no new commits, no file edits since last run), skip the re-run. Add a "last-gate-run" timestamp check before re-queueing.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/CR-00065_S02_run2.log — first pass, all 3 tests pass
ai-dev/logs/CR-00065_S02_run3.log — identical second pass ~6 hours later, all 3 tests pass; nothing changed in worktree between runs
S10 also ran 5 times (run1 fail → run4 pass → run5 pass) — similar spurious re-trigger pattern
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Prevent QV gates from spuriously re-running on unchanged worktrees; add worktree-change detection before re-queueing. analyzed in CR-00065 (see ai-dev/work/CR-00065/reports/CR-00065_self_assess_report.md). Target file: orch/daemon/qv_gate_validator.py. Effort: M.
